functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase
Mediates pro-proliferative signaling upon phosphorylation
Promotes tumor cell proliferation, migration, and invasion
Drug-targetable kinase for antitumor therapy
pathways: Toll-like receptor–reactive oxygen species–EGFR signaling axis
cellular_states: Non-small cell lung cancer (lung adenocarcinoma) cells
pubmed.druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-established druggable tyrosine kinase. Quinazolinone derivatives potently inhibit EGFR (and VEGFR), with SAR studies showing substituents that improve target selectivity and pharmacokinetics; dual-target derivatives can overcome drug resistance. EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (e.g., afatinib) and the bispecific antibody amivantamab are used clinically in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, including uncommon mutations such as G719A. Molecular docking against EGFR PDB 1XKK (e.g., aspidinol binding affinity −8.6 kcal/mol) used to evaluate natural-product inhibitors. EGFR inhibition suppresses microbiota-induced proliferative effects in pancreatic cancer.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase binding EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF)
Ligand-induced receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation on cytoplasmic tyrosines
Recruits adapter protein GRB2 to activate downstream signaling
Phosphorylates RGS16 to activate its GTPase activity, coupling EGFR to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1, increasing its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV
Functions as host cell receptor for virus entry
Role in learning, memory, and pain signaling (hypersensitivity)
uniprot.druggability_notes: ATP-binding tyrosine kinase with nucleotide-binding pocket (ATP-binding, ATP binding GO term), a well-established kinase/transferase drug target class. Proto-oncogene and disease variant keywords indicate therapeutic relevance.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase that binds EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF) to transduce extracellular cues
Ligand binding triggers receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation of cytoplasmic residues
Recruits adapter protein GRB2 to activate downstream signaling cascades
Activates RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK, PI3K-AKT, PLCgamma-PKC and STAT signaling modules
Phosphorylates RGS16 to activate its GTPase activity, coupling EGFR to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1 and increases its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV
Acts as a receptor for hepatitis C virus entry by promoting CD81-CLDN1 complex formation
pathways: Constitutive Signaling by Ligand-Responsive EGFR Cancer Variants (R-HSA-1236382)
Inhibition of Signaling by Overexpressed EGFR (R-HSA-5638303)

disease_associations.disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

cellular_states: neurons (learning, memory and pain signaling, by similarity)
opentargets.druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-validated therapeutic target with kinase activity inhibited by small-molecule tyrosine kinase inhibitors; ligand-responsive cancer variants and EGFRvIII represent oncogenic constitutively active forms. Text notes inhibition of signaling by overexpressed EGFR pathways, consistent with druggability of the cytoplasmic kinase domain.
